summary:
Based on available data, the cross-sectional shape of a lingual bar tends towards being flat; regarding dimensions, while specific gauges vary according to needs, common practices suggest using widths around 6-8 mm for adequate support without excessive complexity. Sentence count: 2
The summary captures both critical aspects—shape preference as simple/flat versus optimal width range—that are essential when considering practical applications within dental prosthodontics related directly back to original query requirements about "cross section" & dimensional specifics pertinent here too given varying preferences noted among professionals discussed above.
